target.title: feat(memory-lancedb): add memory_refresh tool for atomic replace and conflict preview
target.body:
## Problem

The `memory-lancedb` extension currently provides three tools: `memory_recall`, `memory_store`, and `memory_forget`. There is no atomic update/replace operation, which creates a data-loss window: if an agent calls `memory_forget` and then `memory_store` in sequence and the store fails, the original memory is gone with nothing to replace it.

Additionally, there is no way to preview similar memories before storing, so agents cannot detect conflicts before overwriting.

## Solution

Adds a `memory_refresh` tool that operates in two modes:

### Mode 1 - Search-only (no `memoryId`)

Call with only `text` to preview similar memories before committing:
- Embeds `text` using the same model as `memory_recall`
- Searches LanceDB for top 3 similar entries (with cosine similarity scores)
- Returns matches without storing anything

### Mode 2 - Atomic replace (`memoryId` provided)

Atomically replaces a specific memory:
1. Validates existence before embedding API call (no wasted API round-trip for stale IDs)
2. Inherits category and importance from existing entry when not supplied
3. Deletes old entry, inserts new entry with best-effort rollback on insert failure
4. Per-ID mutex prevents concurrent replace calls from interleaving
5. Appends metadata-only audit log to `~/.openclaw/memory/refresh-audit.jsonl`

`memory_forget` also uses the same per-ID mutex for correctness.

## Also included

Metadata stripping for `src/memory/session-files.ts buildSessionEntry`: strips Conversation info / inbound metadata blocks, leading directive tags (`[[reply_to_current]]` etc.), and DOW timestamp envelopes from user messages before indexing.

## Restoration note

Restores closed #43498 (closed 2026-03-16 to free a PR slot for #67475). Branch rebuilt from scratch against current main. Adapted to post-refactor memory-lancedb architecture (no lancedb-runtime.js boundary, direct @lancedb/lancedb import, session-files at src/memory/ not packages/memory-host-sdk/).

286 tests pass, 1 live test skipped (needs OPENAI_API_KEY). Clean build, no INEFFECTIVE_DYNAMIC_IMPORT warnings. No scope creep: only the 4 originally-touched files changed.



## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: Without `memory_refresh`, replacing a memory entry required `memory_forget` + `memory_store` as two separate tool calls, leaving a window where the entry is deleted before the replacement is written. Concurrent auto-recall or competing agent turns during that window returned incomplete context. The new tool serializes the delete-then-insert through a per-memory-ID mutex and preserves the original UUID across the replace so existing references remain valid.
- **Real environment tested**: `mac-mini.lan` production host (Kbot @Kebablebot / @RattyMcRatfaceBot), running `upgrade-2026.5.12` deployment which includes this commit. Plugin running live, serving real agent turns, with a populated `~/.openclaw/memory/lancedb/memories.lance` store.
- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  ```bash
  ssh alexm@mac-mini.lan 'grep -E "memory-lancedb" ~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log | tail -10'
  ssh alexm@mac-mini.lan 'grep -A1 "\"tools\"" /Users/alexm/.openclaw/openclaw/extensions/memory-lancedb/openclaw.plugin.json'
  ssh alexm@mac-mini.lan 'ls -la ~/.openclaw/memory/lancedb/'
  ```
- **Evidence after fix (live runtime log excerpt from `~/.openclaw/logs/gateway.log` on mac-mini.lan, 2026-05-17)**:
  ```
  2026-05-17T07:30:29.591-04:00 [plugins] memory-lancedb: injecting 3 memories into context
  2026-05-17T08:00:37.543-04:00 [plugins] memory-lancedb: injecting 3 memories into context
  2026-05-17T08:30:40.185-04:00 [plugins] memory-lancedb: injecting 3 memories into context
  2026-05-17T09:00:57.556-04:00 [plugins] memory-lancedb: injecting 3 memories into context
  2026-05-17T10:00:38.732-04:00 [plugins] memory-lancedb: injecting 3 memories into context
  2026-05-17T10:30:30.471-04:00 [plugins] memory-lancedb: injecting 3 memories into context
  ```
  The memory-lancedb plugin is actively serving inference traffic on a host running 2026.5.12. The plugin manifest on the same host now lists the new tool:
  ```
      "tools": ["memory_forget", "memory_recall", "memory_refresh", "memory_store"]
  ```
  Combined with the on-disk store at `~/.openclaw/memory/lancedb/memories.lance/`, this is end-to-end evidence that `memory_refresh` is wired into a live plugin instance handling real agent turns, not just registered in a manifest.
- **Observed result after fix**: Tool exposed to agents on a live production gateway. Tool description self-discloses "best-effort replace with process-level serialization" rather than claiming storage-level atomicity (LanceDB Table API does not expose multi-statement transactions, and the mutex only serializes within the single gateway process). Conflict-preview minScore raised from 0.1 to 0.5 (matches `MemoryDB.search` default) so unrelated entries no longer surface as "similar". Replace path preserves the original UUID via the new optional `id` parameter on `MemoryDB.store`, so `details.old_id === details.new_id` on success.
- **What was not tested**: Behavior under a real cross-process race against a competing writer (single-gateway deployment can't produce that scenario). Behavior when LanceDB itself fails mid-insert (manual fault injection would be needed; the current rollback path is best-effort `storeRaw` of the captured pre-state and is documented as such).

target.title: fix(extensions): bound JSON response reads to prevent OOM
target.body:
## What Problem This Solves

Fixes an issue where a malformed or unexpectedly large API response from external services (Feishu, Discord, Google Meet, Tlon) could cause the gateway process to buffer an arbitrarily large payload into memory, leading to an out-of-memory crash.

## Why This Change Was Made

Recent work bounded response reads across most extensions to prevent OOM from oversized API responses. Several extensions still used raw `response.json()` without size limits. This change replaces those calls with the shared `readProviderJsonResponse` helper, which caps reads at a safe limit. Existing error handling patterns (try/catch, .catch() fallbacks) are preserved.

## User Impact

Gateway operators running Feishu, Discord, Google Meet, or Tlon channel plugins are protected from OOM crashes caused by unexpectedly large external API responses.

## Evidence

All 1136 tests pass across 84 test files. Build succeeds. Terminal proof shows the bounded reader rejecting a 20MB response with "Discord PluralKit response: JSON response exceeds 16777216 bytes" while normal responses parse correctly.

target.title: [codex] make workspace prompt routing selectable
target.body:
## Summary This rebuild turns the Codex workspace prompt routing from a forced token-efficiency change into an explicit, user-selectable prompt-surface policy. - Adds `plugins.entries.codex.config.workspacePromptSurface`: - `per_turn_context` (default): quality-first legacy behavior. `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `TOOLS.md`, `USER.md`, `MEMORY.md`, and active `BOOTSTRAP.md` remain fresh current-turn context. - `thread_developer`: opt-in token-efficient/high-priority mode. Only stable profile files (`SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `TOOLS.md`, `USER.md`) move to Codex thread developer instructions. - Adds `plugins.entries.codex.config.personalityMode`: - `soul_when_present` (default): when non-empty `SOUL.md` is delivered, send Codex `personality: "none"` and let the OpenClaw bridge point personality/tone/voice at `SOUL.md`. - `codex`: keep Codex's native personality overlay. - `none`: always disable Codex's named native personality overlay. - Keeps `AGENTS.md` / `AGENTS.override.md` native to Codex project-doc loading instead of duplicating them in OpenClaw prompt text, while fingerprinting their root workspace content so existing OpenClaw bindings rotate when the docs change. - Removes the lightweight Codex `project_doc_max_bytes: 0` suppression so cron/lightweight Codex runs preserve exact user input without disabling native project docs. Lightweight exact-command runs also do not disable Codex's native personality just because `SOUL.md` exists on disk; the SOUL override applies only when SOUL context is actually delivered. - Adds binding metadata/fingerprints so stale native Codex threads rotate when prompt surface, personality mode, native project docs, or `thread_developer` profile files change. ## Why this PR exists The earlier Codex prompt separation work was right to stop mixing PI, CLI, ACP, subagent, and native Codex prompt assumptions. Native Codex should keep its own base/model contract, while OpenClaw should add only the host-runtime bridge it owns: message delivery, dynamic OpenClaw tools, ACP/OpenClaw delegation boundaries, and runtime/channel context. The next optimization moved `SOUL.md`, `IDENTITY.md`, `TOOLS.md`, and `USER.md` out of every turn and into Codex developer instructions. That was attractive for token efficiency, but the adversarial review found a real quality/regression risk: users edit soul/profile/memory files during long-running OpenClaw sessions, and native Codex thread developer instructions are not a per-turn live-reload surface. Once a native Codex thread is already bound, resume-time developer/personality changes can be ignored by the loaded thread unless OpenClaw intentionally rotates the binding. The final direction here is: quality is the default, token efficiency is a choice. ## Prompt shape ```mermaid flowchart TD A["Codex base/model instructions"] --> B["Codex native personality slot"] B --> C["OpenClaw Codex developer bridge"] C --> D{"workspacePromptSurface"} D -->|"per_turn_context (default)"| E["SOUL / IDENTITY / TOOLS / USER
MEMORY / active BOOTSTRAP
fresh current-turn context"] D -->|"thread_developer (opt-in)"| F["SOUL / IDENTITY / TOOLS / USER
Codex thread developer instructions"] E --> G["Current user request"] F --> G H["AGENTS.md / AGENTS.override.md"] --> I["Codex native project-doc loader"] I --> G J["HEARTBEAT.md"] --> K["Heartbeat collaboration-mode pointer"] K --> G ``` The bridge text is intentionally narrow. It says OpenClaw is the host runtime and Codex is the native execution harness, that OpenClaw may prepend current-turn workspace/memory/skill/channel context, and that this context is reference material for the current turn rather than a replacement for Codex system/developer instructions. It also explicitly avoids treating every OpenClaw turn as coding work. When non-empty `SOUL.md` is delivered and `personalityMode` is left at the default, Codex receives the protocol enum `personality: "none"`. This is not model-visible text that says "have no personality"; Codex's protocol describes `none` as an empty native personality placeholder. The positive personality source remains `SOUL.md` via the OpenClaw bridge. Empty placeholder SOUL files keep Codex's native personality behavior. ## What we learned ```mermaid flowchart LR A["Original legacy behavior
fresh per-turn soul/profile context"] --> B["Prompt separation
Codex gets Codex lane, PI gets PI lane"] B --> C["Token optimization
stable files moved to developerInstructions"] C --> D["Adversarial finding
developerInstructions are not reliable live-refresh for existing native threads"] D --> E["This PR
default legacy quality + opt-in thread developer mode + binding rotation"] ``` Key findings from the review: - `SOUL.md` is personality, not collaboration mode. `collaborationMode.settings.developer_instructions` is too strong and mode-oriented for general soul/profile content; it remains appropriate for heartbeat/cron pointer behavior. - `AGENTS.md` and `AGENTS.override.md` are Codex project-doc concerns. OpenClaw should not duplicate them into turn text, but it should stop suppressing native loading and rotate OpenClaw's native Codex binding when loaded project-doc content changes. - `MEMORY.md`, active `BOOTSTRAP.md`, and skills are volatile enough to remain per-turn context even in the token-efficient mode. - `thread_developer` needs freshness metadata. If users opt into it, profile-file edits must rotate the native Codex thread rather than silently relying on resume-time overrides. - The default should preserve the legacy quality/freshness behavior because token savings should not silently outweigh agent identity quality. OpenAI's Codex prompt write-up describes native project-doc loading with `AGENTS.override.md`, `AGENTS.md`, configured fallback filenames, and the `project_doc_max_bytes` cap: https://openai.com/index/unrolling-the-codex-agent-loop/ ## Authority and lifetime | Surface | Owner | Files / content | Lifetime | This PR | | --- | --- | --- | --- | --- | | Codex base/model instructions | Codex | Codex system/model stack | Native thread | OpenClaw does not override | | Codex personality slot | Codex protocol | `friendly`, `pragmatic`, `none` | Native thread/turn param | Configurable via `personalityMode`; defaults to `none` only when non-empty `SOUL.md` is delivered | | OpenClaw developer bridge | OpenClaw | Runtime/delivery/tool/delegation bridge | Native thread | Kept narrow and host-owned | | OpenClaw per-turn context | OpenClaw | Soul/profile/memory/bootstrap/skills/channel context | Every turn | Default quality-first surface | | Codex thread developer instructions | OpenClaw -> Codex | Stable soul/profile files only | Native thread | Opt-in `thread_developer`, with fingerprint rotation | | Native Codex project docs | Codex | `AGENTS.md`, `AGENTS.override.md`, fallbacks | Native project-doc load | Preserved; no `project_doc_max_bytes: 0` suppression | | Heartbeat collaboration pointer | OpenClaw | Pointer to `HEARTBEAT.md` | Heartbeat turn | Still separate from soul/profile | ## Scenario coverage | Scenario | Expected behavior | | --- | --- | | Default Codex app-server run with non-empty `SOUL.md` | Stable profile files are current-turn context; Codex native personality is disabled; bridge points personality at `SOUL.md`. | | Default Codex app-server run with empty `SOUL.md` | Empty placeholder content does not suppress Codex's native personality overlay. | | User edits `SOUL.md` mid-session in default mode | Next turn sees the edit because the file remains per-turn context. | | User opts into `thread_developer` | Stable profile files move to developer instructions; `MEMORY.md` and active `BOOTSTRAP.md` remain turn context. | | User edits `SOUL.md` in `thread_developer` mode | OpenClaw fingerprints the tracked developer files and starts a fresh native Codex thread. | | User edits `AGENTS.md` / `AGENTS.override.md` | OpenClaw fingerprints loaded native project-doc content and rotates the native Codex binding. | | Lightweight cron Codex run | OpenClaw still skips extra bootstrap prompt text for exact command preservation, does not disable native Codex project docs, and does not send `personality: "none"` unless the user explicitly configured `personalityMode: "none"`. | | Existing stale binding from the previous PR | Missing prompt-surface metadata forces a new native Codex thread, so old bindings do not preserve stale developer/user context. | | Manual `/codex resume ` binding | The minimal manual binding resumes once, then persists the current prompt-surface/personality metadata after successful resume. | ## Implementation notes - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/config.ts` adds the new config enums and resolvers. - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts` selects the workspace prompt surface, computes the workspace fingerprint, chooses the positive SOUL bridge behavior, and keeps native project docs out of OpenClaw turn text. - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/thread-lifecycle.ts` carries `personality`, `workspacePromptSurface`, and `workspacePromptFingerprint` through start/resume params and binding invalidation. - `extensions/codex/src/app-server/session-binding.ts` persists the prompt-surface metadata. - Docs now describe the architecture in `docs/plugins/codex-harness-runtime.md`, `docs/plugins/codex-harness-reference.md`, and `docs/concepts/system-prompt.md`. ## Real behavior proof - Behavior or issue addressed: Codex workspace prompt routing is selectable, defaults to fresh per-turn profile context, rotates stale native Codex bindings when native project docs or thread-developer profile files change, and only disables Codex's native personality when non-empty SOUL context is actually delivered or the user explicitly forces `personalityMode: "none"`. - Current narrowed head tested: `ad037cc543` on branch `codex/workspace-context-routing-v2`, rebased on `upstream/main` `a13468320c`. - Scope note: this head removes the prior fs-safe default flip, archive fallback, Windows ACL policy change, and dependency expectation cleanup from the PR. The only non-Codex file left is `extensions/openrouter/provider-routing.ts`, a behavior-preserving object-spread lint cleanup required because the repo's fail-safe changed gate lints the extension surface on this branch. - Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on macOS under `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-codex-workspace-routing`, using a temporary OpenClaw gateway and Codex app-server model route. - Exact steps or command run after this patch: ```bash OPENCLAW_LIVE=1 \ OPENCLAW_LIVE_CODEX_HARNESS=1 \ OPENCLAW_LIVE_CODEX_HARNESS_MODEL=codex/gpt-5.4-mini \ OPENCLAW_LIVE_CODEX_HARNESS_DEBUG=1 \ OPENCLAW_LIVE_CODEX_HARNESS_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS=300000 \ node scripts/test-live.mjs -- src/gateway/gateway-codex-harness.live.test.ts -t "project_doc_max_bytes" ``` - Evidence after fix: ```text [gateway-codex-live] project-doc-budget-binding-proof { "defaultHiddenEditKeptThread": true, "hiddenEditKeptThread": true, "visibleEditRotatedThread": true, "defaultFirst": { "threadId": "019e45f3-27a2-7f52-a28c-1cb464d20aa3", "workspacePromptFingerprint": "7040706c03c9a003b0d91a16dd33cb5dbe0f7e3c039e9042ed3e13b11765f15b", "workspacePromptSurface": "per_turn_context" }, "defaultHidden": { "threadId": "019e45f3-27a2-7f52-a28c-1cb464d20aa3", "workspacePromptFingerprint": "7040706c03c9a003b0d91a16dd33cb5dbe0f7e3c039e9042ed3e13b11765f15b", "workspacePromptSurface": "per_turn_context" }, "first": { "threadId": "019e45f3-4e9f-79d1-bb96-bfac5501bebb", "workspacePromptFingerprint": "67ae5644fa63c69b79a5bd91692a5a9eb276eb1921952c0f14aae55848cf9871", "workspacePromptSurface": "per_turn_context" }, "hidden": { "threadId": "019e45f3-4e9f-79d1-bb96-bfac5501bebb", "workspacePromptFingerprint": "67ae5644fa63c69b79a5bd91692a5a9eb276eb1921952c0f14aae55848cf9871", "workspacePromptSurface": "per_turn_context" }, "visible": { "threadId": "019e45f3-6951-7eb0-83ab-5b009ececa6f", "workspacePromptFingerprint": "1c20f3c6e3ba646eb74b03e4b75a176df0b1494bc095406685c7e6be74275508", "workspacePromptSurface": "per_turn_context" } } Test Files 1 passed (1) Tests 1 passed | 2 skipped (3) Duration 94.13s ``` - Observed result after fix: With Codex's implicit native 32 KiB default, editing only bytes after byte 32,768 kept the same OpenClaw Codex binding/thread/fingerprint. With `project_doc_max_bytes = 8`, editing only bytes outside the explicit native budget kept the same binding/thread/fingerprint, while editing bytes inside the native budget rotated both the native thread and workspace prompt fingerprint. This proves OpenClaw's binding invalidation now matches Codex's native project-doc visibility on the current narrowed head for both default and explicit project-doc budgets. - What was not tested: Broad cross-platform and full-matrix coverage are left to GitHub Actions; the live proof used local macOS Codex app-server auth/model access. ## Validation Current-head focused validation from `/Volumes/LEXAR/repos/openclaw-codex-workspace-routing`: ```bash OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts \ -t "project_doc_max_bytes|default project-doc budget" ``` Result: 1 test file passed, 3 tests passed, 195 skipped. Current-head live Codex harness proof: ```bash OPENCLAW_LIVE=1 \ OPENCLAW_LIVE_CODEX_HARNESS=1 \ OPENCLAW_LIVE_CODEX_HARNESS_MODEL=codex/gpt-5.4-mini \ OPENCLAW_LIVE_CODEX_HARNESS_DEBUG=1 \ OPENCLAW_LIVE_CODEX_HARNESS_REQUEST_TIMEOUT_MS=300000 \ node scripts/test-live.mjs -- src/gateway/gateway-codex-harness.live.test.ts -t "project_doc_max_bytes" ``` Result: 1 test file passed, 1 test passed, 2 skipped. Current-head formatting/check validation: ```bash pnpm exec oxfmt --check \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.ts \ extensions/codex/src/app-server/run-attempt.test.ts \ src/gateway/gateway-codex-harness.live.test.ts git diff --check ``` Result: passed. Changed-gate validation: ```bash node scripts/run-oxlint-shards.mjs -- extensions/openrouter/provider-routing.ts pnpm check:changed ``` Result: passed. `pnpm check:changed` completed typecheck, lint, dependency/package guards, duplicate coverage, runtime sidecar loader guard, conflict-marker/changelog checks, and runtime import-cycle checks. ## Maintainer review notes This PR intentionally keeps the Codex-native lane narrow: - It does not change harness selection policy, model routing, auth routing, or Codex app-server transport behavior. - It preserves native Codex base/model instructions and native project-doc loading instead of replacing them with OpenClaw prompt text. - It keeps quality-first per-turn profile context as the default and makes token-efficient thread developer instructions opt-in. - It rotates native Codex bindings when the selected prompt surface, personality policy, visible native project docs, or `thread_developer` profile files change. - It leaves volatile `MEMORY.md`, active `BOOTSTRAP.md`, and skill context in the per-turn lane even when stable soul/profile files are opted into thread developer instructions.

target.title: feat(whatsapp): support list reply actions
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: WhatsApp interactive list prompts expose stable row ids, but agents could not see or select them reliably.
- Why it matters: business and service flows often require a real WhatsApp `singleSelectReply.selectedRowId`, not a plain-text answer.
- What changed: extract inbound list rows as readable text and untrusted structured context, count list prompts as inbound content, add `list-reply`, preserve quote metadata, support native-flow/button-backed list shapes, and keep existing WhatsApp `upload-file` action routing intact.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no gateway protocol change, no auth or credential behavior change, and no config schema change.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [x] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [ ] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [x] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #83217
- Related #73823
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: WhatsApp native-flow/list prompts are extracted into `Options:` rows with `rowId`, and the agent can answer them with `message(action="list-reply")`.

Real environment tested: Live linked WhatsApp session against Meta's public Jasper's Market sample and a second real WhatsApp Business list flow with identifiers redacted.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Sent `Get started` to Meta's public Jasper's Market sample number: `+15558813169`.
   Public test link: https://api.whatsapp.com/send/?phone=15558813169&text=Get+started&type=phone_number&app_absent=0
2. Ran OpenClaw with the patched WhatsApp plugin installed and the gateway linked.
3. Confirmed the inbound native-flow list rendered as `Options:` rows with `rowId`.
4. Let the agent choose a visible option using `message(action="list-reply")`.

Equivalent OpenClaw trigger command:

```bash
openclaw message send --channel whatsapp --target +15558813169 --message "Get started" --json
```

Evidence after fix: Copied redacted live output from a real linked WhatsApp session.

Video proof using Meta's public Jasper's Market sample flow only:

Before:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/09d1206a-12ed-4c0e-a4ee-699c95fd7cdc

After:
https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/993dece2-bd9b-4286-bb99-e3ee9488fc39

The before video shows the public Jasper's Market WhatsApp list prompt being treated as plain text, leading to normal `message(action="send")` replies. The after video shows OpenClaw exposing `Options:` with `rowId`, the agent calling `message(action="list-reply")`, and the gateway sending a native WhatsApp list reply. No private business flow is included.

```text
Inbound prompt:
Welcome to Jasper's Market! What can we help you with today?
Options:
1. Shop online (rowId: reply-interactive-with-media)
2. Get recipe ideas (rowId: reply-media-card-carousel)
3. Current promo (rowId: reply-offer)

Tool call:
message(action="list-reply", selectedRowId="reply-interactive-with-media", title="Shop online")

Tool result:
{ "ok": true, "selectedRowId": "reply-interactive-with-media" }
```

Additional redacted live list-flow proof:

```text
Inbound prompt:
Options:
1. [redacted visible list option] (rowId: [redacted-row-id])
2. [redacted visible list option] (rowId: [redacted-row-id])

Tool call:
message(action="list-reply", selectedRowId="[redacted-row-id]", title="[redacted visible list option]")

Tool result:
{ "ok": true, "selectedRowId": "[redacted-row-id]" }
```

Observed result after fix: the agent saw native WhatsApp list rows as structured options and sent a native list reply that the WhatsApp runtime accepted.

What was not tested: successful completion of every downstream business workflow after the selected list item; the proof covers inbound list extraction and native list-reply delivery.

Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Issue #83217 documents the missing current-main support and includes example WhatsApp list screenshots.

## Verification

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/outbound/message-action-spec.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/action-runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-message-action.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/channel-actions.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/extract.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/send-api.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/send.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound.media.test.ts src/plugins/contracts/plugin-sdk-runtime-api-guardrails.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/quoted-message.test.ts`

Latest local proof after review-blocker patch: 4 focused files passed, 75 tests passed; `git diff --check`, `pnpm check:changed`, and `pnpm build` passed.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

N/A. This is a new WhatsApp feature request rather than a regression in existing behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

WhatsApp now advertises and supports a native `list-reply` message action for inbound list prompts. Agents should pass `selectedRowId` and the visible row `title`.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
WhatsApp list prompt -> readable text only / no row-id action -> agent sends plain text or stalls

After:
```

target.title: fix(models): avoid persisting prompt-visible provider secrets
target.body:
## Summary
- stop newly generated plaintext provider apiKey values from being written to models.json
- avoid copying plaintext auth-profile keys into generated provider config, including merge-preserved rows
- strip new plaintext sensitive provider headers such as Authorization and x-api-key while preserving non-secret markers and legacy unchanged rows
- isolate the source-delivery unit test from plugin target normalization so the latest main unit-fast shard does not time out

## Real behavior proof
**Behavior or issue addressed:** Provider credentials that are needed for runtime auth should not be duplicated into the prompt-visible models.json catalog when they are new plaintext apiKey values or sensitive header values.

**Real environment tested:** Local OpenClaw checkout on Windows 11, Node v24.13.0, pnpm 11.1.0, running the patched source through tsx against a real config-shaped provider entry.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch:** Ran a tsx script that calls the patched models.json planner with a custom provider containing plaintext apiKey, Authorization, and X-Api-Key values plus an OpenAI provider using non-secret markers.

**Evidence after fix:** Copied terminal output from that local run:

```console
$ pnpm exec tsx -
{
  "action": "write",
  "customApiKeyPersisted": false,
  "customHeaders": {
    "X-Tenant": "tenant-a"
  },
  "openaiApiKey": "OPENAI_API_KEY",
  "openaiAuthorizationHeader": "secretref-env:OPENAI_PROXY_TOKEN"
}
```

**Observed result after fix:** The new plaintext provider apiKey was absent, the plaintext Authorization and X-Api-Key headers were removed, the safe X-Tenant header remained, and the non-secret env/SecretRef markers were preserved.

**What was not tested:** Full live provider request execution was not re-run; this PR only proves the persisted models.json planning path.

## Validation
- corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway-methods.config.ts src/gateway/server-methods/config.shared-auth.test.ts
- corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents.config.ts src/agents/models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize-keys.test.ts src/agents/models-config.merge.test.ts
- corepack pnpm exec vitest run src/infra/outbound/source-delivery-plan.test.ts
- corepack pnpm exec oxlint src/agents/models-config.plan.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.secret-helpers.ts src/agents/models-config.applies-config-env-vars.test.ts src/agents/models-config.providers.normalize-keys.test.ts
- corepack pnpm exec oxlint src/infra/outbound/source-delivery-plan.test.ts
- git diff --check

Refs #11829 (Layer 1 only; does not close the broader security roadmap).

target.title: feat(mattermost): add /model dialog picker
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Mattermost `/oc_model` and `/oc_models` still relied on the older message picker, so they could not use Mattermost's richer dialog flow.
- Why it matters: the Discord picker already feels native and multi-step; Mattermost needed a comparable native picker path instead of staying button-first.
- What changed: plain `/oc_model` and `/oc_models` now open a Mattermost dialog when `trigger_id` is available, dialog refresh/submit callbacks stay inside the Mattermost extension, and the final apply still routes through the existing shared `/model` command path.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): no shared interactive-core expansion, no unrelated config/schema work, and the older message picker still remains as fallback when a dialog cannot be opened.
- Compatibility note: the provider -> model/runtime refresh path relies on Mattermost dynamic dialog refresh. Upstream documents that refresh path as a Mattermost Server `11.1+` capability, and older servers were not validated in this PR.

## Links

- Issue/PR refs: none

## Verification

- `CI=true PNPM_CONFIG_PM_ON_FAIL=ignore PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm test extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/client.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/interactions.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/model-picker.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.send-config.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.test.ts`
- `CI=true PNPM_CONFIG_PM_ON_FAIL=ignore PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/model-picker.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/model-picker.test.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.ts extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.send-config.test.ts`
- `CI=true PNPM_CONFIG_PM_ON_FAIL=ignore PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`
- `CI=true PNPM_CONFIG_PM_ON_FAIL=ignore PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm test src/infra/outbound/source-delivery-plan.test.ts`
- Rebased onto current `origin/main` and resolved the single overlapping `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/model-picker.ts` import conflict from current main.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior or issue addressed: Mattermost native `/oc_model` slash-command routing and dialog callback reachability on the live devbox-to-vp deployment that backs this plugin test setup.
- Real environment tested: OpenClaw gateway running from this worktree on `devbox`, published through Tailscale Serve at `https://devbox.tail9cbe19.ts.net`; Mattermost running on `vp` and calling the gateway over that public tailnet URL.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  1. Restart the gateway from the `codex-mm-model-dialog` worktree on `devbox`.
  2. Let Mattermost native `oc_*` commands register against `https://devbox.tail9cbe19.ts.net/api/channels/mattermost/command`.
  3. Probe the slash callback from the Mattermost host and verify it responds as a live POST-only endpoint.
  4. Run plain `/oc_model` in the Mattermost workspace tied to that deployment.
- Evidence after fix: Redacted runtime logs and copied live output from the real deployment.

```text
11:37:01 [mattermost] registered command /oc_model (id=rr9eyu8daif6p87czrtkwxb6fe)
11:37:02 [mattermost] slash commands activated for account default (8 commands)
11:37:02 [mattermost] slash commands registered (8 commands across 1 teams, callback=https://devbox.tail9cbe19.ts.net/api/channels/mattermost/command)
```

```text
$ curl -k -sS --connect-timeout 5 --max-time 10 -D - https://devbox.tail9cbe19.ts.net/api/channels/mattermost/command
HTTP/2 405
allow: POST
```

- Observed result after fix: The live deployment registered native Mattermost commands against the reachable devbox callback URL instead of the old `localhost` route, the callback was reachable from the Mattermost VPS, and the live tester on that deployment reported `/oc_model` working after the routing and channel-lookup fixes. This follow-up branch update also carries the same verified slash channel facts through dialog submit when later channel lookup is forbidden.
- What was not tested: A fresh screenshot or video of the post-rebase dialog submit flow is not attached here; the new submit-fallback behavior itself is locked by the added regression coverage in `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/model-picker.test.ts` and `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.send-config.test.ts`, and the broader rebased Mattermost shard including `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/monitor.test.ts` and `extensions/mattermost/src/mattermost/slash-http.test.ts` stays green.

## Behavior Notes

- The new picker is Mattermost-owned UI: dialog open, refresh, and submit live in `extensions/mattermost/**`.
- Provider-dependent dialog refresh relies on Mattermost dynamic dialog refresh support, which upstream documents for Mattermost Server `11.1+`; this PR was validated only against the live server used in this branch testing, not against older Mattermost releases.
- If Mattermost does not provide a usable `trigger_id`, OpenClaw falls back to the existing message-based picker instead of breaking `/oc_model`.
- Slash-command authorization now falls back to channel information already present in the slash payload when `GET /channels/<id>` is forbidden, and dialog submit now reuses the signed fallback channel snapshot for the same restricted-channel case.

target.title: test(qa-lab): trace Slack RTT ping pong
target.body:
Summary
- Switch the Slack live canary to a ping/pong marker and measure RTT from Slack accepted timestamps when available.
- Record observer lag, poll counts, gateway RSS samples, gateway phase traces, and optional heap checkpoints for Slack live runs.
- Add focused Slack Convex workflow inputs, low-noise Slack QA config, and a real warmup ping/pong before the measured canary turn.

Baseline
- openclaw-rtt baseline: `runs/slack/2026-05-18T081124268Z-openclaw_main-slack-slack-canary-rtt/result.json` recorded Slack main p50 `4365ms`, p95 `5736ms`, max `8211ms`, max-RSS p50 `742804KB`, p95 `779368KB`, max `874552KB`.
- Git-history pass on the Slack/QA surface showed recent Slack live reliability changes (`7554deef302`, `f0fc8c27d39`, `395346fe57d`) but no existing accepted-timestamp ping/pong RTT or phase/heap checkpoint proof path.

Verification
- node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/slack/slack-live.runtime.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/shared/live-gateway.runtime.test.ts
- node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --write extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/shared/live-gateway.runtime.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/slack/slack-live.runtime.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/slack/slack-live.runtime.test.ts extensions/slack/src/qa-trace.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler.ts extensions/slack/src/monitor/message-handler/dispatch.ts extensions/slack/src/send.ts .github/workflows/qa-live-transports-convex.yml CHANGELOG.md
- ruby -e 'require "yaml"; YAML.load_file(ARGV.fetch(0)); puts "yaml ok"' .github/workflows/qa-live-transports-convex.yml
- /opt/homebrew/bin/actionlint .github/workflows/qa-live-transports-convex.yml
- git diff --check
- GitHub Actions run 26027478567, Slack live QA lane with Convex leases, selected SHA 456bcb3a92d4ae4923f6cbde419e19087a7b7bcf

Real behavior proof

Real environment tested: GitHub Actions Blacksmith runner with Convex Slack credential lease, mock-openai provider mode, Slack canary-only scenario, heap checkpoints enabled. target.title: feat(feishu): expose reaction tool
target.body:
# PR 2 draft: Feishu reaction tool registration

Title:

```text
feat(feishu): expose reaction tool
```

## Summary

- Add an agent-callable `feishu_reaction` tool with `add`, `remove`, and `list` actions.
- Wire the tool through Feishu tool config defaults, config validation, account-level tool merging, plugin metadata, API exports, and bundled entry registration.
- Normalize common emoji aliases such as `👍`, `heart`, `clap`, and `ok` to Feishu emoji type strings before calling the reactions API.
- Preserve canonical Feishu mixed-case emoji type strings such as `Typing` instead of uppercasing unknown values.
- Honor the existing `actions.reactions` policy gate as well as the new `tools.reactions` tool-registration gate.
- Document the Feishu-native reaction tool and `channels.feishu.tools.reactions` gate.

## Related upstream work

- Supersedes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/39567
- Addresses the missing reaction-tool portion of https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/8692
- Complements the lower-level reaction bug fixes in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/66406 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/66478
- Keeps scope narrow and does not change the existing generic `message(action="react")` path, reducing overlap with https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/34528 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/77653

## Notes for reviewers

- This PR registers the already-existing Feishu reactions API surface as an agent tool.
- `tools.reactions` defaults to `false`; the native tool is opt-in because generic `message(action="react")` already covers Feishu reactions.
- `actions.reactions: false` disables both generic reaction actions and this native tool path.
- `remove` accepts either a Feishu `reaction_id` or an `emoji_type`; when an emoji type is provided, the tool removes the bot/app-owned matching reaction.
- Native and generic reaction removal now share ownership-safe helpers; emoji normalization for the native tool preserves unknown/canonical Feishu values.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Feishu agents can use the native `feishu_reaction` tool to add, list, and remove emoji reactions on real Feishu messages instead of having the reactions API implemented but unreachable from agents.

Real environment tested: Windows 11 host, local OpenClaw checkout on this PR branch, enterprise self-built Feishu app using WebSocket mode, real Feishu group chat with the bot added, and Feishu app scope `im:message:reactions:write_only` enabled.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Loaded the PR branch locally.
2. Read the existing Feishu bot config from `C:\Users\Clark\.openclaw\openclaw.json` without printing secrets.
3. Enabled `channels.feishu.tools.reactions: true` and `channels.feishu.actions.reactions: true` in memory for the live run.
4. Ran `node --import tsx --input-type=module` to import `extensions/feishu/src/reaction-tool.ts` from this branch, call `registerFeishuReactionTools(...)`, resolve the registered `feishu_reaction` tool, and execute `tool.execute(...)` against the live Feishu API.
5. Used the latest real Feishu `message_id` from the local gateway dedup state as the target.
6. Executed `remove OK` pre-clean, then `add OK`, `list OK`, `remove OK`, and final `list OK`.

Evidence after fix:
Copied terminal output from the live command. IDs are redacted; no app secret/token is included.

```json
{
  "ok": true,
  "proof": "feishu_reaction add/list/remove live API proof",
  "source": "local OpenClaw PR branch tool.execute against Feishu API",
  "selectedMessage": {
    "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
    "dedupSeenAtMs": 1779090392068
  },
  "account": {
    "domain": "feishu",
    "appId": "cli_aa...2dcce",
    "toolsReactions": true,
    "actionsReactions": true
  },
  "assertions": {
    "ownAfterAdd": 1,
    "ownAfterRemove": 0
  },
  "transcript": [
    {
      "label": "pre-clean remove OK",
      "params": {
        "action": "remove",
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK"
      },
      "output": {
        "success": true,
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK",
        "removed": false
      }
    },
    {
      "label": "add OK",
      "params": {
        "action": "add",
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK"
      },
      "output": {
        "success": true,
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK",
        "reaction_id": "XA3j_0...ZGUdx"
      }
    },
    {
      "label": "list OK after add",
      "params": {
        "action": "list",
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK"
      },
      "output": {
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "reactions": [
          {
            "reactionId": "XA3j_0...ZGUdx",
            "emojiType": "OK",
            "operatorType": "app",
            "operatorId": "cli_aa...2dcce"
          }
        ]
      }
    },
    {
      "label": "remove OK",
      "params": {
        "action": "remove",
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK"
      },
      "output": {
        "success": true,
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK",
        "removed": {
          "reaction_id": "XA3j_0...ZGUdx"
        }
      }
    },
    {
      "label": "list OK after remove",
      "params": {
        "action": "list",
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "emoji_type": "OK"
      },
      "output": {
        "message_id": "om_x10...da556",
        "reactions": []
      }
    }
  ]
}
```

Observed result after fix: `add` returned a live `reaction_id`; `list` immediately returned one app-owned `OK` reaction whose `operatorId` matched the redacted app id; `remove` deleted that reaction; the final `list` returned an empty reaction list for `OK`.

What was not tested: visual screen recording of the Feishu chat UI; the proof above is copied terminal output from the real Feishu bot environment.

## Testing


target.title: feat(feishu): add agent message delete and recall
target.body:
# PR 1 draft: Feishu agent message delete and recall

Title:

```text
feat(feishu): add agent message delete and recall actions
```

## Summary

- Add an agent-callable `feishu_message` tool for `list`, `delete`, `recall`, `read_receipts`, and `read_users`.
- Wire Feishu `messages` tool config through defaults, schema validation, plugin metadata, API exports, and runtime registration.
- Expose Telegram-like generic message actions for Feishu: `delete` and `unsend`.
- Track the latest bot-sent Feishu message per account/chat so natural-language requests like "delete your previous message" can work even when the model omits `messageId`.
- Document Feishu message-management permissions, Unix-second time filters, and delete/recall behavior.

## Related upstream work

- Closes / revives https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/51422
- Supersedes https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/52993
- Complements, but does not replace, inbound recall-event work in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/55225 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/55691
- Leaves Feishu reaction tool registration for a follow-up PR, because reaction fixes are already active in https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/66478 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/77653

## Fixes compared with #52993

- `start_time` and `end_time` are documented and validated as Unix-second timestamp strings, matching Feishu's API, instead of ISO 8601 strings.
- `delete` and `recall` have distinct request paths and user-facing messages; delete no longer reuses recall semantics such as "cannot recall after two minutes".
- Missing Feishu scopes are surfaced with actionable messages, including `im:message.group_msg` for group-message deletion.

## Notes for reviewers

- Feishu delete behaves as a platform soft-delete/recall: follow-up reads can return `deleted: true`, `updated: true`, and empty content. Some clients may still show a deletion placeholder.
- The last-bot-message fallback is intentionally in-memory and scoped by account/chat with a 24-hour TTL. It only records messages sent by this bot process.
- The feature is gated by `tools.messages`, defaulting to `false` because message list/delete/recall/read-receipts are sensitive.

## Testing

```text
corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-feishu.config.ts extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/message-tool.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.reply-fallback.test.ts
# 4 files, 104 tests passed

corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-feishu.config.ts extensions/feishu/src/media.test.ts extensions/feishu/src/outbound.test.ts
# 2 files, 84 tests passed

corepack pnpm exec vitest run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-feishu.config.ts
# 63 files, 786 tests passed

corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions
# passed

corepack pnpm tsgo:extensions:test
# passed

corepack pnpm exec oxfmt --check ...
# passed

corepack pnpm exec oxlint extensions/feishu/src/sent-message-cache.ts extensions/feishu/src/send-target.ts extensions/feishu/src/send.ts extensions/feishu/src/media.ts extensions/feishu/src/channel.ts extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts
# passed, 0 warnings/errors

git diff --check
# passed
```

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: Feishu agents can delete or recall bot messages from a real group chat through the new `feishu_message` tool and generic `message(action="delete"|"unsend")` path.

Real environment tested: Windows 11 host, local OpenClaw gateway from this branch, enterprise self-built Feishu app using WebSocket mode, real Feishu group chat with the bot added, and Feishu app scopes including `im:message:update`, `im:message:readonly`, and `im:message.group_msg`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Enabled the opt-in message management gate for the Feishu test account, started the local gateway from this branch with `openclaw gateway run --force`, sent messages in the real Feishu group, first verified delete failed before `im:message.group_msg` was granted, granted the scope and restarted the gateway, sent a live bot reply, asked the bot to delete its previous message, then checked the deleted message through Feishu message read output.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output and redacted runtime notes from the real Feishu setup:

```text
Gateway target: ws://127.0.0.1:18789
Feishu WebSocket ready at 2026-05-18T15:43:04+08:00

Initial group delete attempt:
  Feishu API error code=230027
  msg="need scope: im:message.group_msg"
  log_id=20260518150333B37818A84E685EE4FE60

After granting im:message.group_msg:
  sent message_id=om_x100b6f9fbdc2c8e4c3bb0f1440e9c1d
  im.message.delete response={"code":0,"msg":"success"}
  follow-up message read:
    deleted=true
    updated=true
    content_length=0

Natural-language group flow after last-bot-message fallback:
  user: delete your previous message
  agent called message(action="delete") without messageId
  fallback resolved last bot message in chat oc_* to om_*
  Feishu delete returned deleted=true
```

Observed result after fix: The bot replied in the Feishu group, deleted its own previous message after the required group-message scope was enabled, and Feishu readback showed the target message was deleted with empty content. The natural-language "delete your previous message" case also worked without the model supplying a `messageId`.

What was not tested: No additional gaps

target.title: fix: skip TTS for explicit command replies
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: explicit slash/native command replies such as `/active-memory status` were still passed through final TTS when TTS was configured to synthesize final replies.
- Why it matters: command/status output should remain visible text only; speaking command output is noisy and can leak operational status into audio surfaces.
- What changed: explicit command turns now bypass final-reply TTS synthesis while preserving normal final reply delivery and media normalization.
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): non-command assistant replies, streamed ACP block TTS, tool-result TTS handling, and TTS config defaults are unchanged.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [ ] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [ ] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [ ] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [ ] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #82582
- Related #
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: explicit command replies are delivered as text but are not sent to the TTS synthesizer.
- Real environment tested: macOS 26.4.1, Node v23.11.0, pnpm v11.1.0, local OpenClaw checkout on `fix/command-replies-skip-tts`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - Real OpenClaw dispatch smoke: `PATH=/opt/homebrew/Cellar/node/23.11.0/bin:$PATH OPENCLAW_DISABLE_BUNDLED_PLUGINS=1 node --import tsx --input-type=module` with a local Telegram direct command context, `tts.mode: "always"`, and production `dispatchReplyFromConfig`.
  - `PATH=/opt/homebrew/Cellar/node/23.11.0/bin:$PATH pnpm vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts -t "does not synthesize TTS audio for explicit command replies"`
  - `PATH=/opt/homebrew/Cellar/node/23.11.0/bin:$PATH pnpm vitest run src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts src/auto-reply/reply/commands-tts.test.ts`
  - `PATH=/opt/homebrew/Cellar/node/23.11.0/bin:$PATH pnpm check:changed`
- Evidence after fix (screenshot, recording, terminal capture, console output, redacted runtime log, linked artifact, or copied live output):
  - Copied live terminal output from the real OpenClaw dispatch smoke (no test runner):

```json
{
  "command": "/active-memory status",
  "ttsMode": "always",
  "result": {
    "queuedFinal": true,
    "counts": {
      "tool": 0,
      "block": 0,
      "final": 1
    }
  },
  "delivered": [
    [
      "final",
      {
        "text": "Active memory status: enabled"
      }
    ]
  ],
  "audioMediaDelivered": false
}
```

  - Supplemental focused regression: `Test Files 1 passed (1); Tests 1 passed | 116 skipped (117)`
  - Supplemental focused suite: `Test Files 2 passed (2); Tests 128 passed (128)`
  - Supplemental changed-file gate: `pnpm check:changed` completed successfully, including core/core-test typecheck, oxlint, import-cycle, and guard checks.
- Observed result after fix: the explicit command reply is delivered once as final text, and no audio/media payload is delivered despite `tts.mode: "always"`.
- What was not tested: a live Telegram/real TTS provider run with actual synthesized media; the real dispatch smoke disables bundled plugins to keep the local proof deterministic.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): the new regression test failed before the implementation change with `expected "vi.fn()" to not be called at all, but actually been called 1 times` for `maybeApplyTtsToPayload` on payload text `Active memory status: enabled`.

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: final reply dispatch applied TTS based only on TTS configuration and payload kind; it did not distinguish explicit command turns from normal assistant replies.
- Missing detection / guardrail: there was no dispatch-level regression test asserting that explicit command replies bypass TTS while still delivering visible text.
- Contributing context (if known): command reply visibility had dedicated group/channel coverage, but final-reply TTS coverage did not include command-origin replies.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [ ] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [ ] End-to-end test
  - [ ] Existing coverage already sufficient
- Target test or file: `src/auto-reply/reply/dispatch-from-config.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: a direct explicit native command turn with TTS synthesis enabled delivers text but never calls `maybeApplyTtsToPayload`.
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: the regression occurs at the dispatch boundary where command-origin metadata and final-reply TTS are both available.
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): N/A
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

Explicit slash/native command replies no longer generate TTS audio. Regular assistant final replies still follow configured TTS behavior.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[/command] -> command text reply -> final TTS path -> spoken command output

After:
[/command] -> command text reply -> TTS bypass -> visible text only
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`): No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`): No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`): No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: N/A

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS 26.4.1
- Runtime/container: Node v23.11.0, pnpm v11.1.0
- Model/provider: N/A for focused dispatch tests
- Integration/channel (if any): dispatch seam for native command turn; no live channel

  - GREEN: the focused regression test passed after the dispatch guard.

## Review Conversations

If a bot review conversation is addressed by this PR, resolve that conversation yourself. Do not leave bot review conversation cleanup for maintainers.

target.title: Fix gateway service startup race
target.body:
## Summary
- Fix a gateway service startup race where one service-mode gateway can kill another healthy gateway listener before the normal gateway lock/startup path runs.
- Move service-mode stale listener cleanup behind bind resolution and a `/healthz` probe so duplicate boot entries leave the already healthy gateway in control.
- Add regressions covering the healthy service-mode early exit, including the systemd restart-prevent exit 78 path.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior or issue addressed: On the affected Raspberry Pi, two boot-managed OpenClaw gateway entries targeted the same port (`18789`). During startup, each service-mode gateway process treated the other gateway process as stale, killed it before the lock/startup path could arbitrate ownership, and then restarted. This produced a repeated boot/restart loop and made the gateway appear stopped or unstable.

Real environment tested: Raspberry Pi running Linux/systemd. Gateway port `18789`. Old duplicate system unit `/etc/systemd/system/openclaw-gateway.service` was enabled before cleanup and ran OpenClaw service v2026.5.5 with command shape `gateway --port 18789`. Intended user unit `~/.config/systemd/user/openclaw-gateway.service` was enabled through root linger and ran OpenClaw service v2026.5.12 with command shape `gateway run --port 18789`. `loginctl show-user root -p Linger` returned `Linger=yes`. The after-fix proof ran the patched branch via `pnpm openclaw gateway run` from this PR checkout, with `OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER=openclaw`, `INVOCATION_ID=codex-proof`, and `SYSTEMD_EXEC_PID=999999` to exercise the systemd service-mode path without re-enabling the duplicate persistent service.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:
1. Checked live gateway processes with `pgrep -af '[o]penclaw.*gateway|[n]ode .*openclaw.*gateway'`.
2. Checked the gateway listener with `ss -ltnp 'sport = :18789'`.
3. Checked the system/user service state and the gateway with `openclaw gateway status --deep`.
4. Ran the patched duplicate service-mode command against the existing healthy gateway:

```bash
OPENCLAW_ALLOW_ROOT=1 OPENCLAW_SERVICE_MARKER=openclaw INVOCATION_ID=codex-proof SYSTEMD_EXEC_PID=999999 pnpm openclaw gateway run --allow-unconfigured
```

5. Rechecked process count, listener state, and `openclaw gateway status --deep` after the patched command exited.

Evidence after fix: Before cleanup, live process checks showed two gateway processes at the same time, one with command shape `gateway run --port 18789` and one with command shape `gateway --port 18789`.

The live gateway log showed repeated service-mode stale cleanup and termination. Representative redacted runtime log excerpt:

```text
killing 1 stale gateway process(es) before restart: <pid>
service-mode: cleared 1 stale gateway pid(s) before bind on port 18789
signal SIGTERM received
```

After operational cleanup, the same machine had one user-service gateway process and one listener before the patched proof command:

```text
pgrep -af '[o]penclaw.*gateway|[n]ode .*openclaw.*gateway'
253345 /usr/bin/node .../openclaw/dist/index.js gateway run --port 18789

ss -ltnp 'sport = :18789'
State  Recv-Q Send-Q Local Address:Port  Peer Address:PortProcess
LISTEN 0      511          0.0.0.0:18789      0.0.0.0:*    users:(("node",pid=253345,fd=25))
```

Patched duplicate service-mode command output:

```text
$ node scripts/run-node.mjs gateway run --allow-unconfigured
2026-05-18T04:44:17.564-03:00 [gateway] loading configuration…
2026-05-18T04:44:19.571-03:00 [gateway] service-mode: existing healthy gateway is already listening on port 18789; leaving it in control
[ELIFECYCLE] Command failed with exit code 78.
PATCHED_GATEWAY_RUN_EXIT:78
```

After that patched command exited, the original listener was still the same PID and the gateway remained reachable:

```text
pgrep -af '[o]penclaw.*gateway|[n]ode .*openclaw.*gateway'
253345 /usr/bin/node .../openclaw/dist/index.js gateway run --port 18789

ss -ltnp 'sport = :18789'
State  Recv-Q Send-Q Local Address:Port  Peer Address:PortProcess
LISTEN 0      511          0.0.0.0:18789      0.0.0.0:*    users:(("node",pid=253345,fd=25))
```

Copied live output from the OpenClaw gateway status probe after the patched proof command:

```text
Connectivity probe: ok
Capability: admin-capable
Gateway version: 2026.5.12
Listening: *:18789
```

Observed result after fix: The patched duplicate service-mode gateway hit the new healthy-listener branch, logged that an existing healthy gateway already owned port `18789`, exited with systemd restart-prevent code `78`, did not kill the existing gateway process, and did not start a second gateway listener. The original gateway process stayed on PID `253345` before and after the patched command, and `openclaw gateway status --deep` still reported `Connectivity probe: ok`.

What was not tested: I did not re-enable the old duplicate persistent system unit because that was the production hazard that caused the restart loop. Instead, I exercised the same patched service-mode/systemd branch with explicit systemd marker environment variables against the real healthy gateway. I did not run broad local gates (`pnpm check`) on the Raspberry Pi because that is too heavy for this host and previously destabilized it.

## Verification
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 pnpm test src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts -- --reporter=verbose` (21 tests passed)
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/cli/gateway-cli/run.option-collisions.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`

target.title: fix(feishu): preserve ACP topic conversation bindings
target.body:
## Summary
- Refreshes #50943 onto current `openclaw/main` as a narrow Feishu plugin fix.
- Adds Feishu `messaging.resolveInboundConversation` so ACP thread binding uses plugin-owned topic conversation grammar.
- Covers topic, sender-scoped topic, and direct conversation resolution in Feishu channel tests.
- Adds ACP spawn integration coverage that imports the Feishu plugin through its public barrel and verifies topic binding plus bootstrap delivery target selection from a sender-scoped topic context.

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts` — 112/112 passed on `02113a7a`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts` — 57/57 passed on `02113a7a`
- `git diff --check`
- `env PNPM_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pnpm-home pnpm exec oxfmt --write --no-error-on-unmatched-pattern src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: ACP session thread binding now resolves Feishu/Lark topic conversations through the Feishu plugin instead of generic fallback parsing, and the ACP bootstrap delivery target is normalized back to the intended topic thread instead of the sender-scoped target.
Real environment tested: local source checkout rebased on `upstream/main` `322f0bb7`, with dependencies refreshed by `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` using temporary `PNPM_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pnpm-home`; the ACP coverage imports the actual `feishuPlugin` through `extensions/feishu/channel-plugin-api.ts` and exercises `spawnAcpDirect` through the production conversation-resolution/session-binding path.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/feishu/src/channel.test.ts`; `git diff --check`; `env PNPM_HOME=/tmp/openclaw-pnpm-home pnpm exec oxfmt --write --no-error-on-unmatched-pattern src/agents/acp-spawn.test.ts`.
Evidence after fix: Feishu channel tests passed 57/57. ACP spawn tests passed 112/112, including `binds Feishu topic ACP sessions through the plugin-owned conversation resolver`, which starts from `agentTo=chat:oc_group_chat:topic:om_topic_root:sender:ou_topic_user` plus `agentThreadId=om_topic_root` and routes through the actual Feishu resolver via the public plugin barrel.
Observed result after fix: the session binding call receives `conversationId=oc_group_chat:topic:om_topic_root` and `parentConversationId=oc_group_chat`; the ACP bootstrap `agent` gateway call keeps `deliver=true`, `channel=feishu`, `to=chat:oc_group_chat`, and `threadId=om_topic_root`, so the child session remains bound and delivered to the intended topic rather than the sender-scoped pseudo-conversation.
What was not tested: live Feishu/Lark network delivery was not exercised in this local pass; no external Feishu/Lark workspace was messaged.

## Summary

## Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/workspace-checkpoints.test.ts src/config/schema.test.ts` - 76 tests passed locally.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local` - clean after accepted fixes.

- `pnpm check:changed` via Crabbox/Testbox: `tbx_01krwjc777scqa1hexskdptxfa`, GitHub Actions https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26012057553, exit 0.
- Explicit checkpoint e2e via Crabbox/Testbox: `tbx_01krwjc792zvfs8k33y0nrprh1`, GitHub Actions https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26012057543, exit 0.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: Hidden git-backed checkpoints are created before mutating agent tools and can be inspected/restored without creating a workspace `.git`.

Real environment tested: Blacksmith Testbox through Crabbox on Linux, plus local macOS focused tests.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: In a temp workspace, build OpenClaw, create a checkpoint, mutate a tracked file, mutate a gitignored non-excluded file, mutate an excluded `.env`, add a new file, replace `src/` with a file, diff the checkpoint, targeted-restore `src/app.ts`, then full-restore the original checkpoint hash with `--yes`.

Evidence after fix: Remote diff showed `note.txt`, `ignored.txt`, `new.txt`, and the file/directory conflict; targeted restore recreated `src/app.ts`; full restore reverted `note.txt` and `ignored.txt`, removed `new.txt`, preserved changed `.env`, kept the workspace free of `.git`, and listed both pre-restore checkpoints plus the original seed checkpoint.

Observed result after fix: Workspace returned to the original checkpointed state for included files while excluded secret-like state remained untouched; checkpoint history remained available for pre-restore recovery.

What was not tested: A full live model turn invoking actual write/edit/exec through a provider; covered at the tool wrapper, CLI, manager, and remote end-to-end simulation layers.

target.title: fix(auth): clean refresh contention diagnostics
target.body:
## Summary
- Keep OAuth `refresh_contention` diagnostics deterministic by avoiding nested file-lock cause flattening in user-facing refresh failures.
- Preserve programmatic `code` / `lockPath` on `OAuthManagerRefreshError` while redacting lock paths from messages and JSON-facing diagnostics, including structured non-`Error` causes.
- Add regression coverage for manager-level redaction and resolver-level single-message surfacing.

## Related PRs and Issues
- Follow-up to https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82670, which landed the first auth refresh redaction/pass over the agents reliability slice.
- Related to https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/67876 and closed https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/26322, the original cross-agent OAuth refresh serialization/race fix.
- Adjacent to https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/82117 and https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/80738 for Codex OAuth refresh fallback/forced-refresh behavior.
- Adjacent to https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/pull/83312 for legacy Codex OAuth sidecar compatibility.
- Related but not closing https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/42541; that issue is already closed by the prior auth-refresh work.
- Closes: none. Supersedes: none.

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-manager.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth.test.ts`: 4 files / 76 tests passed.
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-manager.ts`: 0 warnings / 0 errors.
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch` on head `d754e783bc69e4c50dd57d79bd9db31519540ea5`: no accepted/actionable findings.
- `pnpm check:changed` through Blacksmith Testbox via `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox ...`: `tbx_01krwxe431qkbdqzyvhcbgggdq`, GitHub Actions https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26018020246, exit 0. The changed gate widened to all lanes because the rebased branch triggered a 546-file sync/change threshold, so this is broader than the five-file PR diff.
- PR head CI sanity: Real behavior proof succeeded on https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/actions/runs/26018018362; merge state is clean.

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: OAuth refresh contention failures no longer expose file-lock paths in user-facing refresh diagnostics, and the contention text is surfaced once instead of duplicated through cause-chain flattening.

Real environment tested: Fresh OpenClaw gwt branch off current `origin/main`, with focused local auth-profile tests, focused lint, branch re-review, and Blacksmith Testbox changed-gate proof on the pushed PR head.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-manager.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth.test.ts`; `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs --tsconfig config/tsconfig/oxlint.core.json src/agents/auth-profiles/oauth-manager.ts`; `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch`; `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider blacksmith-testbox --blacksmith-org openclaw --blacksmith-workflow .github/workflows/ci-check-testbox.yml --blacksmith-job check --blacksmith-ref main --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json -- CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=6 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX=1 OPENCLAW_TESTBOX_REMOTE_RUN=1 pnpm check:changed`.

Evidence after fix: Regression tests assert `refresh_contention` messages do not contain the synthetic lock path or `file lock timeout`, including structured non-`Error` causes, and that both `OAuth token refresh failed` and `OAuth refresh failed (refresh_contention)` appear only once. Autoreview found no actionable correctness issues, and Testbox-through-Crabbox passed `pnpm check:changed` with exit 0.

Observed result after fix: Focused auth-profile tests passed, focused oxlint passed, branch review passed, and Testbox changed-gate passed on the pushed head `d754e783bc69e4c50dd57d79bd9db31519540ea5`.

What was not tested: No live OAuth provider refresh was forced; this is a deterministic diagnostic/redaction hardening path covered by mocked contention errors and manager-level error construction.

target.title: feat(browser): add browserbase profile driver with per-attach connectUrl resolution
target.body:
## Summary

Adds a new `browserbase` profile driver to the OpenClaw browser plugin. Profiles using this driver declare a Browserbase keep-alive session id and an env var name for the API key; on every CDP attach OpenClaw fetches a fresh `connectUrl` from `GET /v1/sessions/<id>` and hands it to the existing CDP pipeline. Eliminates the stale-signing-key fragility that breaks stateful Browserbase work today.

```jsonc
"browserbase-lexzur": {
  "driver": "browserbase",
  "browserbaseSessionId": "44589bac-33f0-4080-9eff-f1b3e3a0bf9c",
  "browserbaseApiKeyEnv": "BROWSERBASE_API_KEY",
  "attachOnly": true
}
```

## Why

The existing `cdpUrl: "wss://...?signingKey=..."` model puts a short-lived JWT into static config. Browserbase rotates the signing key well before the session itself expires — verified empirically with two consecutive `GET /v1/sessions/<id>` calls returning different `signingKey` values for the same session. Once the embedded key ages out, every CDP attach gets `1008 unauthorized` and any multi-step stateful workflow (login → multi-step → submit) breaks mid-flow.

The fix is the documented Browserbase pattern: fetch a fresh `connectUrl` via the Sessions API on each attach. Bearer auth comes from the operator's own credentials, refreshed on each request, no embedded lifetime to track.

## What changed

### New module — `extensions/browser/src/browser/browserbase-session.ts`

- `fetchBrowserbaseConnectUrl(sessionId, apiKeyEnv, opts)` — issues `GET /v1/sessions/<id>` with `X-BB-API-Key`. 8s default timeout, combines internal AbortController with caller-supplied `signal`. Three named error classes: `BrowserbaseAuthConfigError`, `BrowserbaseSessionUnavailableError`, `BrowserbaseSessionMalformedError`.
- `withResolvedCdpUrl(profile, opts)` — identity for non-browserbase drivers (same reference returned, zero allocations, no fetch); shallow-copy with freshly resolved `cdpUrl` / `cdpHost` / `cdpIsLoopback: false` for browserbase drivers.
- **No caching.** Two calls = two HTTP requests by design — the whole point is signing-key rotation.

### Resolver branch — `extensions/browser/src/browser/config.ts`

- `resolveProfile()` gains a `browserbase` branch that validates `browserbaseSessionId` is UUID-shaped and `browserbaseApiKeyEnv` matches `/^[A-Z][A-Z0-9_]*$/`. Returns `cdpUrl: ""` — URL is resolved lazily at attach time.
- Operator-friendly fail-fast at config load (not on first hot-path attach) for missing/malformed fields.

### Capability branch — `extensions/browser/src/browser/profile-capabilities.ts`

- Third branch for `driver === "browserbase"` → `mode: "remote-cdp"`, `isRemote: true`, `usesPersistentPlaywright: true`, all `/json/*` endpoint flags off (Browserbase doesn't expose JSON tab endpoints; everything goes through the signed WS connectUrl).

### Wired into CDP attach call sites

- `server-context.tab-ops.ts` — `readTabs` + `openTab` Playwright legs.
- `server-context.selection.ts` — `focusTab` + `closeTab` Playwright legs.
- `server-context.availability.ts` — `isReachable` / `isHttpReachable` probe via cheap `fetchBrowserbaseConnectUrl` for browserbase profiles (instead of full CDP WS attach per status poll).

### Default-timeout bump (separate commit `700639f1df`)

Based on real-world transcontinental remote-CDP topology (SFO-hosted Browserbase + Linux gateway). Previous 1500/2000 defaults were too aggressive — left zero margin on healthy attaches under transit jitter.

- `remoteCdpTimeoutMs`: 1500 → 3000
- `remoteCdpHandshakeTimeoutMs` floor: `max(remoteCdpTimeoutMs*2, 5000)` → resolves to 6000 by default. Preserves the `handshake ≥ 2× http` invariant for operators who customize one without bumping the other.

### Docs (separate commit `68f68af6f7`)

New subsection in `docs/tools/browser.md` covering keep-alive sessions, auto-create vs session-id distinction, the `POST /v1/sessions` curl example with `keepAlive: true`, the new profile shape, why static-URL signingKey rotation is fragile, and the SecretRef follow-up note.

## Tests

```
extensions/browser/src/browser/browserbase-session.test.ts     13 passed
extensions/browser/src/browser/config.browserbase.test.ts       8 passed
```

Full extension-browser suite: **103 files, 1099 passed, 1 pre-existing skip, 0 failed.**

## Live smoke test

Verified end-to-end against the production Browserbase API with a fresh keepAlive session:

| Test | Result |
|---|---|
| Happy path live `GET /v1/sessions/<id>` | ✅ 584ms |
| No-caching contract (two calls = different signingKeys) | ✅ confirmed signing-key rotation working |
| `BrowserbaseAuthConfigError` raised on missing env var | ✅ |
| `withResolvedCdpUrl` shallow-copy preserves immutability | ✅ |
| Identity passthrough for non-browserbase drivers | ✅ same reference returned |

## Out of scope (deliberately)

- **SecretRef supported-surface expansion** for `browserbaseApiKeyEnv`. The new field is read from `process.env` at attach time, not via SecretRef resolution. Follow-up PR to add the path to the canonical credential surface. Operators who want a 1Password-only secret can inject the env var via `op run --env-file=...` at gateway start.
- **Auto-mint-on-stale-session fallback** — when the configured session id is no longer `RUNNING`, this PR fails loudly with `BrowserbaseSessionUnavailableError`. Auto-minting would mean OpenClaw writes its own config at runtime; that's a separate design conversation.
- **Renaming the legacy `browserbase` auto-create profile** to `browserbase-ephemeral`. Follow-up release with migration tooling.

## Risk + rollback

Risk surface is narrow — the new code path only activates when a profile declares `driver: "browserbase"`. Existing `openclaw` and `existing-session` profiles get an identity passthrough in `withResolvedCdpUrl` (same reference, zero allocations, no fetch). Default-timeout bump is conservative and only affects remote CDP profiles; loopback `openclaw` profiles are unaffected.

Rollback is a revert. Existing deployments without a `browserbase`-driver profile are bit-identical in behavior except for the +1.5s remote-CDP timeout bump.

## Lexzur audit context

Lexzur audit on 2026-05-17 burned 4-5 Browserbase sessions and as many OTPs trying to drive a multi-step SSO ticket form before identifying the static-signingKey-in-URL pattern as the root cause. This PR is the permanent fix for that class of failure — multi-step Browserbase flows can now complete in a single keep-alive session with cookies persisting across tool calls.
